non_blocking_risks.risk: If governance-checkpoints.json changes after one reviewer has already approved the payload, the approval chain may need to restart from an earlier checkpoint.

same_source_consistency.evidence: The governance topology and the representation payload refer to the same bounded export candidate rather than parallel payload variants.

cross_format_consistency.evidence: xml-rewrite.xml and representation-bundle.json describe the same review object that governance-checkpoints.json routes through a multi-reviewer chain.

fact_validation_chain.item: Confirm xml-rewrite.xml exists as one stable payload candidate.
Confirm representation-bundle.json exposes the same payload and visibility package to every reviewer role.
Confirm governance-checkpoints.json defines review_roles, review_topology, approval_order, required_artifact_visibility, and rejection_reentry_rules.
Confirm override-policy.json defines reviewer_conflict_resolution and export_regeneration_rules.
Confirm final release is gated by governance rather than by representation serialization alone.

unverifiable_assumptions.item: The actual human reviewers will inspect the same payload revision referenced by the current representation-bundle.json.

check.note: governance-checkpoints.json and override-policy.json exist and define multi-reviewer topology, rejection handling, and release authority.
xml-rewrite.xml and representation-bundle.json exist and preserve one stable export candidate across all review steps.

scope_alignment_checks.item: The round stays on human review topology and export authorization logic and does not widen into runtime governance operations.

check.note: The export payload can be prepared early, but it remains only a candidate until the multi-reviewer topology is declared.
governance-checkpoints.json and override-policy.json exist before the payload is treated as releasable.
representation-bundle.json preserves one reviewer-visible payload bundle after governance topology has been declared.

dispatch_recommendations.item: Proceed because payload construction, reviewer topology, rejection handling, and final release authority are explicitly separated.

check.why: Without it, multi-reviewer order, artifact visibility, and rejection paths would remain implicit.
check.evidence: mode-resolution.json places governance above export release eligibility and review topology.

check.why: It preserves the shared review object across all reviewers and prevents topology drift from turning into payload drift.
check.evidence: Both governance-checkpoints.json and xml-rewrite.xml depend on the same bundle-level visibility assumptions.

evaluation_report.score_reason: The example now covers a multi-reviewer export approval topology, although it still models a bounded review chain rather than every possible approval graph shape.
